titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Grenfell Press Collection
titlestmt.author: Kolbe Resnick, Elizabeth Nosari, and Michaela Gibbons.

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 19, 2025 at 23:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Grenfell Press on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund and the Alfred Z. Baker Fund, 2017-2022. Accession 2022-ycal-0023 was a gift of Leslie Miller, 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into three series: Series I. Business files, 1983-2016 Series II. Production files and prints, 1972-2019 Series III. Promotional material, circa 1983-2000

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Grenfell Press, founded by Leslie Miller in 1979, specializes in artists books, limited edition prints, artist portfolios and collaborative publications, with many fine prints and artist books in major collections. Over Grenfell Press's 46 years, the press has collaborated with a wide variety of artists and writers, including John Ashbery, William Burroughs, Robert Creeley, Susan Howe, Ann Lauterbach, Eliot Weinberger, Vija Celmins, Francesco Clemente, Carroll Dunham, Robert Gober, R.B. Kitaj, Robert Mangold, Brice Marden, Frank Moore, Elizabeth Murray, Joel Shapiro, Kiki Smith, Nathaniel Tarn, Fred Tomaselli, Richard Tuttle, and Trevor Winkfield. Experimentation with the print medium and an immense attention to detail is a hallmark of Grenfell Press, resulting in books, prints, and portfolios printed on wide ranges of paper types and using traditional to more experimental printing techniques—from woodblock to lithograph to photogravure.

In addition to running Grenfell Press, Leslie Miller also designs and produces posters, catalogues and museum monographs for institutions including James Cohan Gallery, Detroit Institute of Arts, Gagosian Gallery, Luhring Augustine, Matthew Marks, the Metropolitan Museum of Art, the Museum of Modern Art, New Directions, the New York Public Library, and the Whitney Museum of American Art.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Grenfell Press Collection. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related material: Susan Howe Papers (YCAL MSS 338).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Grenfell Press Collection consists of business materials, correspondence, photographs, prints, artist and printer's proofs, original artwork, typescripts, broadsides, printing blocks, artist books, posters, and ephemera related to and created in the operations of Grenfell Press. Included are production materials for publications, as well as other printed material such as letterhead and greeting cards. The material in the collection spans 1972 to 2021, documenting Grenfell Press' activities as a highly collaborative endeavor, bringing together artists, writers, and printers in the production of every publication.

The collection highlights the breadth of printing techniques and materials Grenfell Press experimented with, from natural Japanese paper to heavy fiber paper, woodblock to lithograph. Illustrated in the collection are the many stages of printing undertaken by Grenfell Press in the process of creating their editions.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Grenfell Press Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by material type, into four subseries: Sub-Series a. Correspondence, 1986-2000 Sub-Series b. Invoices, 1996-2012 Sub-Series c. Appointment books, 1983-2016 Sub-Series d. Printed ephemera and work samples, 1981-2016 Sub-Series e. Photographs, 1987-2010

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I. consists of material created in the operation of Grenfell Press, including invoices for various stages of prints and publications, correspondence with artists, appointment books, photographs of artists and the workspace, as well as examples, proofs, and tests of prints and ephemera commissioned by various galleries and institutions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes typescript for Multitude of One.

did.unittitle: Grenfell Press: 30 Years of Collaboration, exhibition price list,
Museum of Modern Art, Library Council
NYPL, Edwin L. Cox Collection catalogs, proofs

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by artist last name, with collaborative projects arranged into a subseries, alphabetically by project title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II. contains all production materials for publications produced by Grenfell Press. Included in this series are original artworks, printing blocks, stencils, test prints, artist and printer's proofs, as well as numbered final editions.

Grease Bag bag, Grease Bag pin-up, BAT

Tulsa , Teenage Lust , title page proofs

scopecontent.p: linocut prints on coral reef paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III. Includes posters and promotional material created by Leslie Miller, but not necessarily printed by Grenfell Press. This includes oversize posters designed for the Metropolitan Museum of Art, New York Public Library, and other institutions and art galleries.

did.unittitle: Miller, Leslie, posters, Metropolitan Museum of Art
